news.summary: Geekco Technologies Corporation announced its preliminary unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The company reported no revenue for Q4 2024 compared to ($20,000) in the previous year. For the fiscal year 2024, Geekco also reported no revenue compared to $6,819 in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 is projected to be between ($100,000) and ($110,000) compared to ($486,000) for the same period in the previous year. For the fiscal year 2024, adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between ($1,400,000) and ($1,500,000) compared to ($1,830,000) in the previous year. These figures are based on preliminary information subject to revision upon finalizing closing procedures and the audit of the annual financial statements. The detailed article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Geekco Technologies Corp reports no revenue for Q4 2024, Adjusted EBITDA between ($100,000)-($110,000); fiscal year Adjusted EBITDA ($1.4M)-($1.5M).

news.summary: The economic growth showed a slight decrease in the fourth quarter, while the Federal Reserve's measure of inflation remained consistent with recent positive movements. S&P 500 futures experienced an increase following these updates. The GDP growth rate for Q4 slowed down, as reported, and the Federal Reserve's inflation indicator aligned with expectations. Additionally, S&P 500 futures saw an uptick in response to this data.
news.headline: Q4 GDP growth slowed, Fed inflation in check, S&P 500 futures rose.

news.summary: The main inflation rate of the Federal Reserve remains high but has maintained positive readings for the past two months. S&P 500 futures have seen an increase. Concerns ahead of tariffs have been alleviated by favorable Fed inflation and wage data, with the S&P 500 potentially reaching a record high.
news.headline: Fed inflation rate remains elevated, good readings for two months, S&P 500 rises. Tariff concerns ease.

news.summary: The S&P 500 rebounded from earlier lows following President Trump's decision to postpone tariffs on Mexico. The market's initial drop underscores the ambitious nature of his policies. The delay in implementing the tariffs on Mexico was a significant development after the index fell due to concerns over the high-risk nature of the plan. Meanwhile, tariffs on China remain in place, adding to the ongoing trade tensions.
news.headline: Trump delays launch date due to S&P 500 fall after high-risk plan; China tariffs remain, S&P 500 reacts to bold move.

news.summary: The likelihood of a Fed rate cut was impacted by a moderately hawkish jobs report and an increase in inflation expectations. As a result, the chances of a rate cut by the Fed decreased. This news caused the S&P 500 to fall.
news.headline: Hawkish jobs report & inflation rise reduce Fed rate-cut odds; S&P 500 falls post-report.

news.summary: The latest data from the producer price index indicates that inflation is not as severe as indicated by the consumer price index. Despite this, S&P 500 futures are on the rise. However, the looming threat of Trump tariffs is expected to compel the Federal Reserve to maintain its current stance. The post emphasizes that the PPI reveals a continued decline in inflation, contrary to concerns raised by the CPI. This news comes amidst a positive trend in the S&P 500 market.
news.headline: PPI data indicates declining inflation, with S&P 500 rising. Trump tariffs to affect Fed's decisions.

news.summary: Tariffs imposed by Trump are set at 25% and are expected to impact three industries with significant trade deficits starting on April 2. This is in addition to three other upcoming tariff deadlines. These tariffs are targeting industries facing substantial trade imbalances, and the implications are significant as the deadlines approach.
news.headline: Trump tariffs set to hit industries with trade deficits; 25% tariffs by April 2. 4 huge tariff deadlines approaching.

news.summary: Amid threats of Trump tariffs causing turbulence in the S&P 500, there is a glimmer of hope with the Federal Reserve's key inflation gauge, the PCE price index, showing a cooling trend. This comes as spending slows, and the S&P 500 experiences fluctuations. This development offers some reassurance in the midst of economic uncertainties.
news.headline: Fed's PCE price index eases with slowing spending, S&P 500 rises despite Trump tariff concerns.

news.summary: Last month, planned layoffs experienced a significant increase to a level not seen in four years, attributed to the influence of Elon Musk's DOGE. The surge in layoffs has garnered attention, however, it is noteworthy that former President Trump has reportedly removed Musk's ability to make further workforce reductions. This development comes as a surprising twist, signaling a shift in the trajectory of job cuts.
news.headline: Layoffs soar to 4-year high due to Musk's DOGE impact, Trump cancels layoffs tool.

news.summary: In February, there was a moderate increase in employment as the economy began to see the impact of DOGE job reductions and Trump's tariffs later in the month. The labor market displayed some weakness, although Federal Reserve Chair Powell referred to it as stable. Additionally, the S&P 500 experienced a rebound during this period.
news.headline: Feb jobs report indicates soft labor market; Fed Chair Powell deems it 'solid'. S&P 500 rebounds.

news.summary: In February, the consumer price index revealed that core inflation decreased more than anticipated, reflecting consumer hesitancy. Following this, S&P 500 futures surged. The report pointed to a decline in inflation after a decrease in travel related to Trump's policies. This development was noted in an article by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Core CPI inflation in February cooled more than expected; S&P 500 futures jumped. Suggests consumer caution.

news.summary: Jobless claims remain unaffected by the recent layoffs at DOGE. Despite this, concerns about a potential shutdown are looming. The S&P 500 has experienced a slight decline in response to these events. The impact of the DOGE layoffs on initial unemployment claims has not materialized as of yet. The situation is being closely monitored by investors and the broader market.
news.headline: DOGE-related layoffs not impacting initial jobless claims. S&P 500 declines amid shutdown threat.

news.summary: Jerome Powell, the Federal Reserve Chairman, is looking for more information on the Trump administration's plans before considering a potential interest rate cut. However, he may not have the time to wait for complete clarity. The S&P 500 is eagerly awaiting insights from Powell following the Fed meeting. It is essential for investors to hear about the potential rate cut amidst economic uncertainties. Powell's decision will heavily influence the market as it navigates this period of uncertainty.
news.headline: Fed Chair Powell waits for clarity on Trump agenda for rate cut. S&P 500 impatient. - Investor's Business Daily

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, Vornado Realty Trust revealed that its street retail joint venture, in which it holds a 52% stake, successfully secured a $450 million financing for 1535 Broadway in Times Square. This iconic retail destination boasts full blockfront 4K LED signage. The financing, a non-recourse loan, is interest only and comes with a fixed rate of 6.90%. The loan is set to mature in May 2030.
news.headline: Vornado Realty's JV secured $450M financing for 1535 Broadway in Times Square with fixed 6.90% rate, due in May 2030.

news.summary: Federal Reserve officials have forecasted a total of two reductions in interest rates expected to take place in 2025. This decision comes in response to a rise in unemployment rates and a period of temporary inflation caused by tariffs. The Federal Reserve has adopted a cautious approach towards economic growth, with the expectation of mild expansion. Despite these developments, the S&P 500 experienced a surge in performance following the announcement.
news.headline: Fed predicts 2 rate cuts in 2025, amid higher unemployment and 'transitory' inflation, S&P 500 rallies.

news.summary: In February, the inflation gauge preferred by the Federal Reserve increased. This rise occurred just before the implementation of tariffs by the Trump administration. As a result, S&P 500 futures experienced a decline.
news.headline: Fed's preferred inflation gauge increased in February, pre-Trump tariffs; S&P 500 futures declined.

news.summary: In a surprising development, consumer prices experienced an unexpected decrease, with core inflation reaching its lowest point in four years. This decline in inflation occurred simultaneously with a notable drop in the Consumer Price Index due to the impacts of Trump's tariffs on the travel sector. As a result, the S&P 500 witnessed a sharp decline in response to these economic changes.
news.headline: CPI inflation drops as Trump tariffs impact travel; S&P 500 plunges. Overall prices fell, core inflation at four-year low.

news.summary: China's international competitiveness has been significantly influenced by small factories with narrow profit margins. However, these businesses are currently at risk of facing potential disaster.
news.headline: Chinese factories, vital for international competitiveness, are struggling due to Trump's tariffs, risking disaster.

news.summary: Before President Trump abruptly reversed his decision, the bloc had voted to enforce retaliatory actions. Following this, the bloc revealed a postponement in their planned measures.
news.headline: EU postpones retaliation against US tariffs after Trump's reversal. The bloc had voted for measures, but delayed them for talks.

news.summary: A significant number of Republicans have expressed approval regarding the recent surprising decision to halt certain tariffs by President Trump for a period of three months. However, there are individuals within the party who are seeking further clarification regarding the president's ultimate objective in this matter. Additionally, there is a call for increased authority for Congress when it comes to trade-related issues.
news.headline: Pause on Trump's tariffs calms GOP nerves. Three months extension welcomed, questions persist about end game and Congressional power.

news.summary: On Thursday, White House officials provided further details regarding the tariffs announced by the president. They stated that the 125% tariff mentioned earlier was on top of an existing 20% tariff implemented since President Trump's return to office. This brings the total tariff to 145%, marking a significant increase in trade costs for the country involved.
news.headline: White House clarified Trump's addition of 145% tariff to China, includes original 125% and new 20% since his return.

news.summary: In the latest report, it has been revealed that the country's economy grew by a staggering 6.2% in the past year, surpassing all expectations. This growth has been primarily driven by a 10% increase in exports, reaching a record high value of $500 billion. Furthermore, the unemployment rate has plummeted to just 3.5%, the lowest in over a decade. The government has also announced a budget surplus of $100 billion, attributing it to increased tax revenues and prudent fiscal management. Experts are optimistic about the future economic outlook, citing strong consumer confidence and steady growth in key industries.
news.headline: Bank of England takes rare action as bond market sees a sell-off.

news.summary: US President Donald Trump hosted El Salvador’s President Nayib Bukele at the White House on April 14 to discuss trade and immigration, avoiding Bitcoin discussions. Trump proposed transferring US citizens convicted of crimes to El Salvador, prompting the need for more prisons. The Trump administration has deported numerous alleged foreign criminals to El Salvador under a $6 million deal. On April 2, Trump addressed the trade war, suggesting a potential temporary exemption for automakers. El Salvador signed a $1.4 billion loan agreement with the IMF in December, needing to unwind Bitcoin initiatives; however, it continues daily BTC purchases, holding 6,147.18 BTC, valued at about $520.7 million. Trump signed an executive order to create a Bitcoin strategic reserve in the US, holding nearly 198,000 BTC valued at over $17 billion, seized from criminal activities like the Silk Road and Bitfinex hack cases. The meeting didn't delve into digital assets, despite El Salvador’s adoption of Bitcoin as legal tender in 2021.
news.headline: Trump and Bukele discuss trade and immigration at White House, leaving out Bitcoin. US holds over 198,000 BTC valued at $17B. IMF opposes El Salvador's BTC plans.

news.summary: Producers of soybeans are issuing a warning that their farms are at risk of potential closure due to the recent imposition of a significant tariff increase of 145% by the Trump administration on imports from China. This drastic tariff hike has raised concerns within the industry, as it could have detrimental effects on soybean producers, with many fearing that their farms may not be able to sustain operations under these new economic conditions.
news.headline: US farmers at risk as Trump admin imposes 145% tariffs on China, threatening soybean producers with financial ruin.

news.summary: HP Inc., based in Palo Alto, California, shared on April 14, 2025, that it priced its underwritten public offering of $1 billion worth of senior unsecured notes. This offering consists of $500 million in 5.400% notes due 2030 at a public price of 99.732% of the principal amount, and $500 million in 6.100% notes due 2035 at a public price of 99.778% of the principal amount. These combined notes are referred to as the "Notes." HP Inc. trades on the NYSE under the symbol HPQ.
news.headline: HP Inc. priced $1 billion senior unsecured notes, $500M each for 5.400% due 2030 and 6.100% due 2035.
Latest report shows Consumer Discretionary Sector & Industry Performance trends on Bloomberg.

news.summary: On Monday, the stock market experienced significant gains triggered by news of Trump's tariffs, as reflected in Dow Jones Futures. Notably, Netflix and Palantir emerged as top stocks to monitor amidst this rally. The market saw a rise in value, with companies like Spotify also demonstrating strong performance. This positive momentum was underscored by a surge in investor interest, particularly in the highlighted stocks, marking a notable shift in market dynamics due to the tariff news.
news.headline: Dow Jones Futures fall after Trump tariff news, but stock market rallied. Netflix, Palantir, Spotify among best stocks to watch.

news.summary: The company Fomento Económico Mexicano, S.A.B. de C.V. (FEMSA) based in Monterrey, Mexico, has announced its First Quarter Conference Call scheduled for April 14, 2025. Shareholders and investors are invited to participate in this event. FEMSA is listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FMX) and the Mexican Stock Exchange (BMV: FEMSAUBD, FEMSAUB).
news.headline: FEMSA schedules first-quarter financial results conference call on April 14, 2025 (NYSE: FMX; BMV: FEMSAUBD, FEMSAUB).

news.summary: Today, an historic accomplishment was made as the first all-female crew embarked on a journey to space. Amanda Nguyen made history as the first Vietnamese and Southeast Asian woman to venture into space. The momentous event took place in Van Horn, Texas on April 14, 2025. The successful mission was carried out by Blue Origin's NS-31. Space for Humanity celebrated this milestone in space exploration with great joy and anticipation.
news.headline: All-female crew, including Amanda Nguyen, first Vietnamese and Southeast Asian woman, successfully launches on Blue Origin's NS-31 mission from Van Horn, Texas on April 14, 2025.

news.summary: In Toronto on April 14, 2025, WildBrain Ltd. ("WildBrain" or the "Company") announced significant progress in its television broadcast division, which encompasses Family Channel, Family Jr., and WildBrainTV. As a prominent figure in kids' and family entertainment worldwide, the company (TSX: WILD) continues to drive growth in this sector.
news.headline: WildBrain (TSX: WILD) updates on TV channel sale, including Family Channel, Family Jr, and WildBrainTV.

news.summary: HOLLAND, Mich. - Inspired Closets has introduced Envision, a state-of-the-art Augmented Reality (AR) tool, revolutionizing the way customers perceive and adopt home organization systems. This new feature enables users to preview organization solutions in their homes, guaranteeing the right fit and style prior to buying. Many customers struggle with envisioning how organizational products will integrate into their living areas, making Envision a game-changer. With this tool, individuals can accurately assess layouts, enhancing the shopping experience.
news.headline: Inspired Closets debuts AR tool Envision, helping customers visualize home organization solutions; revolutionizing shopping experience.

news.summary: Healthcare Realty Trust Incorporated, listed on NYSE under the symbol HR, is set to release its first quarter financial results for 2025 on Thursday, May 1, 2025, after the market closes. The announcement was made in Nashville, Tennessee, on April 14, 2025, through a press release.
news.headline: Healthcare Realty Trust (NYSE: HR) to report Q1 2025 results on May 1, 2025, after market close.

news.summary: The upcoming AMD EPYC CPU, referred to as "Venice," will be the initial HPC product introduced on TSMC's latest N2 node. This marks a significant milestone in the industry, showcasing the advancement towards next-generation technology. The new CPU is poised to deliver enhanced performance and efficiency, setting a new standard for high-performance computing. This development highlights the growing importance of TSMC's N2 node, signaling a transition towards cutting-edge technology. AMD's decision to unveil the EPYC CPU on this platform underscores their commitment to innovation and staying at the forefront of the market. This move is expected to have a notable impact on the industry, with experts anticipating a surge in demand for the new EPYC CPU. The launch of the "Venice" CPU on the N2 node sets the stage for a new era in high-performance computing, with projections indicating a substantial market share gain for AMD in the coming months. The innovative features and capabilities of this next-generation CPU are projected to revolutionize the HPC sector, offering users a superior computing experience. This development solidifies AMD's position as a key player in the industry and reinforces their reputation for delivering cutting-edge technology solutions.
news.headline: AMD's new EPYC CPU "Venice" achieves milestone as first HPC product on TSMC N2 node.

news.summary: Under a national security statute, the administration has initiated an investigation into the effects of imported semiconductors and pharmaceuticals. This move is aimed at safeguarding critical industries by assessing the implications of foreign imports. The investigation will evaluate the impact on domestic production and assess potential risks associated with reliance on imported goods. This decision underscores the importance of protecting key sectors, with a focus on semiconductors and pharmaceuticals. The administration's action is driven by concerns over national security and aims to ensure the self-sufficiency and resilience of these industries.
news.headline: Administration invoking national security statute to investigate impact of foreign computer chips and drugs, potential new tariffs on imports.

news.summary: According to recent reports, a total of 1,500 homes were destroyed in the recent natural disaster that struck the region. The devastating event also resulted in 10 casualties and left over 500 individuals injured. Additionally, approximately 2,000 people have been evacuated from the affected area and are currently being housed in temporary shelters. The local authorities are working tirelessly to provide relief efforts and support to those impacted by this tragedy.
news.headline: Markets relieved as bond-market rebounds from trade turmoil, easing tensions.

news.summary: A leading technology company has released its latest AI models, showcasing significant advancements in performance. The new models have exceeded their predecessors in terms of efficiency and capability, ultimately reducing costs for the company. However, the complexity of the AI models' names has generated bewilderment and confusion among industry observers. The latest AI models have demonstrated a notable improvement in performance, with a reduction in costs for the company, but have also raised eyebrows due to their perplexing nomenclature.
news.headline: OpenAI unveils GPT-4.1, surpassing predecessors and creating confusion with GPT-4.5, delivering improved performance and lower costs.

news.summary: John H. Johnson has been named as the new Executive Chairman, Todd F. Baumgartner, MD, MPH will serve as the Chief Regulatory Officer, and Reena Thomas Colacot has been appointed as the Vice President and Head of Quality.
news.headline: Applied Therapeutics announces new executive appointments in Q4 2024; includes John H. Johnson as Executive Chairman.

news.summary: In Sacramento, California on April 14, 2025, it was reported that Blue Diamond Growers, the leading almond processor and marketer, released a statement endorsing the recent announcement made by USDA Secretary Rollins regarding the launch of the new initiative called Advancing Markets for Producers (AMP). The company expressed its support for this initiative, highlighting their commitment to aiding producers in accessing new markets.
news.headline: Blue Diamond Growers supports USDA's AMP initiative to boost farmer resiliency. Largest almond processor.

news.summary: Tether, the issuer of USDT, has announced its plans to allocate its current and future computing power to the well-known Bitcoin mining pool, Ocean.
news.headline: Tether backs Bitcoin mining pool Ocean, deploying current and future hashrate to support -USDT issuer commits to support.

news.summary: A global pop culture lifestyle brand, Funko, Inc. (NASDAQ: FNKO), revealed plans to open its first Southeast Asian store in the Philippines with local retailer, Funtastik Enterprises Corp. The store is set to debut in June 2025, marking a significant step in Funko's international expansion strategy. This move underscores Funko's dedication to engaging with fans in their regions and enhancing the brand experience.
news.headline: Funko to open first licensed store in Southeast Asia in the Philippines by June 2025; expanding international growth.

news.summary: In Okotoks, Alberta, on April 14, 2025, Mullen Group Ltd. (TSX: MTL) shared its excitement about finalizing a significant deal. The corporation revealed that it has signed a definitive agreement to purchase the shares of Cole Group Inc., Cole International Inc., Abco International Freight Inc., and associated entities, known as the "Cole Group." The acquisition is contingent upon regulatory approvals, with both parties having submitted the required documents and anticipating the closure of the transaction in the second quarter of the year.
news.headline: Mullen Group Ltd. to acquire Cole Group Inc. including Cole International Inc. and Abco International Freight Inc. pending regulatory approval. Transaction expected to close in the second quarter.

news.summary: Vinci Partners Investments Ltd., with the ticker VINP on NASDAQ, based in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, declared on April 14, 2025, that they are the controlling entity of a top-tier alternative investments and global solutions firm in Latin America.
news.headline: Vinci Compass to release Q1 2025 results on May 12; key info to be shared in Rio de Janeiro.

news.summary: Following a label expansion, UPLIZNA has entered a new rare disease market, yielding fruitful results for Amgen after its acquisition of Horizon Therapeutics for USD 27.8 billion. This drug is now the inaugural FDA-approved treatment for a specific condition.
news.headline: FDA approves Amgen's UPLIZNA for IgG4-related Disease, tapping USD 27.8B rare disease market opportunity.

news.summary: BioSyent Inc., a Canadian company listed on the TSX Venture exchange under the symbol RX, is set to participate in the Planet MicroCap Showcase: VEGAS 2025 investor conference at the Paris Hotel & Casino in Las Vegas, Nevada. The event will take place from April 22 to April 24, 2025. Mr. René Goehrum, the President and CEO of BioSyent, will be delivering a live presentation on April 23rd from 12:30pm to 1:00pm PDT in Track 2 – Bordeaux. This provides attendees with the opportunity to learn more about the company and its operations firsthand.
news.headline: BioSyent attends investor conference in Vegas on April 23, 2025. CEO to present at 12:30 pm PDT in Track 2.

news.summary: In the latest update on the economy, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has decreased to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in the past decade. This improvement in the job market has led to a rise in consumer spending, with retail sales increasing by 6.5% compared to last year. Additionally, the stock market has seen significant growth, with the main index reaching an all-time high of 35,000 points. Analysts are optimistic about the future economic outlook, predicting further growth in the upcoming months.
news.headline: Lawsuit filed by Carbon Streaming against ex-executives and consultants for unspecified damages.

news.summary: Carl Black Orlando in Orlando, Florida, announced on April 14, 2025, that they are offering a low APR for drivers seeking to purchase a new full-size SUV. This limited-time finance offer aims to make vehicle ownership more accessible. Qualified buyers have the opportunity to secure this financing option.
news.headline: Carl Black Orlando is offering 0% APR on select 2024 Chevy models, making ownership more accessible for drivers in Orlando.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, Georgia-Pacific revealed that Keith Hamilton, previously the vice president of operations for Corrugated, has been appointed as the president of the Corrugated division. With oversight of operations and commercial teams spanning 35 locations, Hamilton steps into this new role to lead the business forward.
news.headline: Keith Hamilton, VP of Operations, Corrugated, named president of Georgia-Pacific's Corrugated business overseeing 35 sites.

news.summary: In Wilmington, Delaware, on April 14, 2025, Bannix Acquisition Corp. disclosed that their board of directors approved an extension for the initial business combination deadline. The new deadline date, which was previously set for April 14, 2025, has been moved to May 14, 2025. This extension gives Bannix an extra month to finalize the business combination.
news.headline: Bannix Acquisition Corp. extends deadline to finalize initial business combination to May 14, 2025.

news.summary: On April 14, 2025, Gates Industrial Corporation plc (NYSE: GTES) revealed a shift in the schedule for its first-quarter 2025 webcast and conference call. The event, originally set for 10 a.m. Eastern time, will now take place at 9 a.m. Eastern time on Wednesday, April 30, 2025. Further details regarding access to the webcast and conference call were also provided.
news.headline: Gates Industrial changes Q1 2025 conference call time from 10 a.m. to 9 a.m. on April 30, 2025.

news.summary: The trade wars initiated by President Trump's plan to impose tariffs on US imports have caused concerns in the cryptocurrency markets. Truebit executives believe that blockchain technology can help ensure fair application of tariffs by proving provenance and chain of custody for products. Trump's proposed tariffs would impact $2.4 trillion worth of imports, with the possibility of other countries implementing retaliatory measures. Truebit, a blockchain network, is in talks with software vendors and exploring its potential role in global supply chains. However, there are risks to blockchain networks due to tariffs, including disruptions to physical infrastructure and regulatory obstacles. Truebit's native token has a market capitalization of $20 million. Other crypto executives caution that trade wars could pose challenges to blockchain networks' integrity and user accessibility.
news.headline: Trade wars may drive governments towards Web3 adoption, says Truebit execs. Trump's proposed tariffs could impact $2.4 trillion in imports. Blockchain tech can ensure fair application of tariffs, improving supply chain transparency. Truebit's TRU token struggles post-launch in 2021. Several execs warn of risks to blockchain networks amid escalating trade tensions.
